description: Fix documentation inaccuracies found during a systematic audit of docs against the source code. Corrects wrong secret file paths, outdated dev commands, incorrect pipeline phase counts, missing CLI command aliases, undocumented CLI commands, and incomplete SDK utility docs.
qualityChecks: cd docs && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip && bun run format:check

userStories.title: Fix incorrect secret location and dev command in install page
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the install page to show the correct auth secret location and dev command so that I can follow the setup instructions without errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Line ~143 of install/index.mdx shows `bun run dev` (or `bun run dev:mcp`) instead of `bun --hot platform/mcp-server/dist/index.js --dev`
Line ~153 of install/index.mdx shows `cat ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json | jq -r .secret` instead of `cat ~/.opentabs/config.json | jq -r .secret`
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
cd docs && bun run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx

Line 143 currently shows:
```bash
bun --hot platform/mcp-server/dist/index.js --dev
```
Change to:
```bash
bun run dev
```
This matches the root CLAUDE.md which documents `bun run dev` as the correct full dev mode command.

Line 153 currently shows:
```bash
cat ~/.opentabs/config.json | jq -r .secret
```
Change to:
```bash
cat ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json | jq -r .secret
```
Per root CLAUDE.md: 'The WebSocket secret is stored in ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json as { "secret": "<hex>" }. This is the single source of truth — config.json does not store the secret.'

userStories.title: Fix incorrect secret location, pipeline phase count, and dev command in architecture page
userStories.description: As a contributor reading the architecture docs, I want accurate descriptions of the discovery pipeline, auth secret location, and dev mode commands so that I understand the system correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Line ~77 says 'four-phase pipeline' instead of 'five-phase pipeline'
The five numbered pipeline steps are consolidated to four: resolve (npm + local), load, determine trust tier, build registry
Line ~368 references `~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json` instead of `~/.opentabs/config.json` for the secret location
Line ~473 reference to 'five-phase' discovery pipeline is corrected to 'four-phase'
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
cd docs && bun run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx

1. Line 77 says 'five-phase pipeline'. Per root CLAUDE.md, it's a four-phase pipeline: resolve → load → determine trust tier → build immutable registry. The current docs list resolve-npm, resolve-local, load, merge, build-registry as 5 steps. Consolidate to:
   1. Resolve — scans global node_modules for npm plugins AND reads filesystem paths from config.localPlugins. Local plugins override npm plugins of the same name.
   2. Load — reads package.json, dist/adapter.iife.js, and dist/tools.json for each plugin
   3. Determine trust tier — assigns source-based tier (Official, Community, Local)
   4. Build registry — constructs immutable PluginRegistry with O(1) lookup maps

2. Line 368 says the secret is 'the 64-character hex secret from ~/.opentabs/config.json'. Change to 'the 64-character hex secret from ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json'.

3. Line 473 references 'five-phase plugin discovery pipeline'. Change to 'four-phase'.

userStories.title: Fix dev mode command in configuration page
userStories.description: As a contributor reading the configuration docs, I want the dev mode command to show the correct convenience command so that I use the recommended workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: The dev mode section shows `bun run dev` instead of `bun --hot platform/mcp-server/dist/index.js --dev`
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
cd docs && bun run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx

Line ~304 in the 'Dev mode (contributors only)' section shows:
```bash
bun --hot platform/mcp-server/dist/index.js --dev
```
Change to:
```bash
bun run dev
```
The root CLAUDE.md documents `bun run dev` as the recommended full dev mode command (tsc watch + MCP server + extension, colored output with startup banner). Alternatively `bun run dev:mcp` for server-only work.

userStories.title: Add missing CLI command aliases to CLI reference
userStories.description: As a user reading the CLI reference, I want to see all command aliases documented so that I can use the shorter forms.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs plugin list section mentions the `ls` alias
opentabs plugin install section mentions the `add` alias
opentabs plugin remove section mentions the `rm` alias and the `-y` shorthand for `--confirm`
opentabs config show section mentions the `get` alias
opentabs config rotate-secret section documents the `--confirm` flag
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
cd docs && bun run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx

Source code aliases found in platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts and config.ts:
- Line 648: plugin list has `.alias('ls')`
- Line 664: plugin install has `.alias('add')`
- Line 681: plugin remove has `.alias('rm')`
- Line 684: plugin remove --confirm has `-y` shorthand: `.option('-y, --confirm', ...)`
- Line 574: config show has `.alias('get')`
- Lines 601-603: config rotate-secret has `.option('--confirm', 'Skip confirmation and rotate immediately')`

For each alias, add it to the command heading or description. For example:
- 'opentabs plugin list' → add note like 'Alias: `opentabs plugin ls`' or update the heading
- 'opentabs plugin remove' → add `-y` to the Options table alongside `--confirm`
- 'opentabs config rotate-secret' → add `--confirm` to an Options table

Keep the style consistent with existing command documentation in the file.

userStories.title: Document opentabs-plugin inspect command in CLI reference
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the CLI reference, I want to see the opentabs-plugin inspect command documented so that I know how to inspect my built plugin manifest.
acceptanceCriteria: The Plugin Developer CLI section documents the `opentabs-plugin inspect` command
Documentation includes the `--json` flag
Documentation includes a brief description matching 'Pretty-print the built plugin manifest (dist/tools.json)'
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
cd docs && bun run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx

The CLI reference already documents `opentabs-plugin build` under a 'Plugin Developer CLI' section (or similar). Add `opentabs-plugin inspect` in the same section.

Source: platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/inspect.ts
- Command: `opentabs-plugin inspect`
- Description: 'Pretty-print the built plugin manifest (dist/tools.json)'
- Options: `--json` — 'Output raw JSON instead of formatted summary'
- Requires `dist/tools.json` to exist (run `opentabs-plugin build` first)
- Shows: plugin name, version, SDK version, tool count, resource count, prompt count, and details for each

Note: check how the existing `opentabs-plugin build` command is documented and follow the same pattern. If there's no Plugin Developer CLI section yet, look at the bottom of the file or search for 'opentabs-plugin'.

userStories.title: Add missing maxDelay option to RetryOptions in SDK utilities docs
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the SDK utilities reference, I want to see the maxDelay option documented on RetryOptions so that I know how to cap exponential backoff delays.
acceptanceCriteria: The RetryOptions table in utilities.mdx includes a `maxDelay` row with type `number`, default `30000`, and description about capping exponential backoff delays
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
cd docs && bun run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx

The RetryOptions table (around lines 584-619) is missing the `maxDelay` property. Source: platform/plugin-sdk/src/timing.ts lines 12-13:
```typescript
/** Maximum delay in milliseconds when using backoff, preventing unreasonable wait times (default: 30000) */
maxDelay?: number;
```

Add a new row to the Table between the `backoff` row and the `signal` row:
- Option: `maxDelay`
- Type: `number`
- Default: `30000`
- Description: Maximum delay in milliseconds when using exponential backoff. Prevents unreasonably long wait times.

Follow the exact same Table.Row/Table.Cell pattern used by adjacent rows.

userStories.title: Fix deleteJSON return type in SDK utilities docs
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the SDK utilities reference, I want the deleteJSON signature to show the correct return type so that I handle 204 No Content responses correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: The deleteJSON overload without schema shows return type `Promise<T | undefined>` instead of `Promise<T>`
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
cd docs && bun run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx

Around line 422, the deleteJSON backward-compatible overload shows:
```typescript
function deleteJSON<T>(url: string, init?: FetchFromPageOptions): Promise<T>;
```

Source (platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts line 344-345) shows:
```typescript
<T>(url: string, init?: FetchFromPageOptions): Promise<T | undefined>;
```

The `| undefined` is significant: 204 No Content responses return `undefined` (see fetchJSONImpl line 94). Change the return type to `Promise<T | undefined>`. The example on line 429 (`const result = await deleteJSON<{ ok: boolean }>(...)`) should note that result may be undefined, or use optional chaining.
